subject: UX & Research
title: The Halo Effect
description: A fast breakdown of The Halo Effect for builders.

hook.text: An attractive person is rated smarter and kinder. One positive trait colors all judgments. That's the halo.

buildup.text: Thorndike found it in 1920. Officers rated soldiers as good-at-everything or bad-at-everything, not mixed.

discovery.text: Apple's design quality creates a halo. Users rate Apple's customer service, speed, and value higher than rivals.

twist.text: The halo works in reverse too. One bad experience — slow loading — makes users rate everything lower.

climax.text: First impressions set the halo. Invest in landing pages, onboarding, and first-use experience.

punchline.text: One great trait lifts all. One bad trait sinks all. Nail first impressions.

quiz.question: How does the halo effect work in reverse for products?
options: One bad experience like slow loading makes users rate all aspects — design, content, trust — lower
The reverse halo only affects brand-new users
Bad experiences are always forgotten after good ones
